app.title: Amor Fati Tracker
app.subtitle: "To want nothing other than what is" - Nietzsche

meta.description: Amor Fati self-assessment - Track your philosophical journey
meta.ogDescription: Assess your relationship with life through 5 philosophical dimensions and track your progress toward amor fati.

welcome.heading: Welcome to your Amor Fati journey
welcome.intro: This tool lets you assess where you stand in your journey toward full acceptance of your life (amor fati) and track your progress over time.
welcome.confidentialDesc: All your data stays on your device. Nothing is sent online. You can even use it offline.
welcome.priorityQuestion: Before you begin: which dimension would you like to see improve first?
welcome.prioritySubtitle: This reflection will help guide your inner work. You can change it at any time.

ressentiment.label: 🔥 Past & Resentment
ressentiment.desc: Free myself from the weight of my past, overcome anger and bitterness
ressentiment.short: Free myself from the weight of my past
ressentiment.full: 🔥 Past & Resentment - Free myself from the weight of my past

souffrance.label: ⚡ Present Suffering
souffrance.desc: Better accept and navigate current difficulties
souffrance.full: ⚡ Present Suffering - Better accept current difficulties

authenticite.label: 🎭 Authenticity
authenticite.desc: Live by my own values, truly be myself
authenticite.full: 🎭 Authenticity - Live by my own values

creation.label: 🎨 Creation
creation.desc: Become an active creator of my life rather than merely react
creation.short: Become an active creator of my life
creation.full: 🎨 Creation - Become an active creator of my life

eternel.label: ♾️ Eternal Return
eternel.desc: Fully affirm my life, wish to relive it exactly as it is
eternel.full: ♾️ Eternal Return - Fully affirm my life

none.label: 🧭 No specific priority
none.desc: I just want to observe my overall progress
none.full: 🧭 No specific priority - Observe my overall progress

assessment.selectPriorityAlert: Please select a priority before starting.
assessment.contextPlaceholder: What's going on in your life right now? (e.g., hard week at work, calm period, significant event...)

results.focusDimension: Focus on {{dimension}}: this is your weakest dimension ({{score}}/8). That's where the work will have the most impact.
results.evolutionContinue: Keep assessing yourself regularly to see your progress over time.

history.emptyDesc: Start your first assessment to track your progress.
history.chartEmpty: Complete at least 2 assessments to see your progress.

language.title: 🌐 Interface language
language.desc: Choose the display language of the application.

settings.exportDesc: Download all your assessments as a JSON file to back them up or transfer them to another device.
settings.importDesc: Restore your assessments from a backup file. You can choose to replace or merge your data.
settings.tipDesc: It's recommended to assess yourself once a week, on the same day (e.g., Sunday evening) for better comparability.
settings.deleteWarningDesc: This action is irreversible. All your assessments will be permanently deleted.

data.invalidAssessment: Invalid assessment data in the file
data.importBody:
How would you like to import the data?

Replace: current data ({{existing}} assessment(s)) will be deleted.
Merge: imported assessments will be added to existing ones (duplicates are ignored).
data.deleteConfirm1:
Are you sure you want to delete ALL your data?

This action is irreversible!
data.deleteConfirm2: Final confirmation: all your assessments will be permanently lost.

priorityModal.title: Change my priority
priorityModal.body: Which dimension would you like to see improve first?

pwa.installDesc: Install Amor Fati Tracker on your device for quick access and offline use.

offline.title: Offline — Amor Fati Tracker
offline.heading: 🔌 Offline
offline.desc: You are offline. You can review your latest locally saved assessments below.
offline.footer: When you're back online, reload the page to sync and get the latest version.

1.title: 1. Relationship to the past & Resentment
1.description: How do you relate to your personal history and the difficult events you've experienced?

2.title: 2. Attitude toward present suffering
2.description: How do you respond to the current difficulties and suffering in your life?

3.title: 3. Relationship to self & Authenticity
3.description: To what extent do you live by your own values rather than external expectations?

4.title: 4. Creation vs Reaction
4.description: Are you an active creator of your life or do you mainly react to circumstances?

5.title: 5. The Eternal Return test
5.description: Nietzsche's ultimate question: would you relive your life exactly as it has been?

q1.text: When you think back on difficult events in your past, what do you mainly feel?
q1.opt0: Vivid anger, a sense of injustice that regularly consumes me
q1.opt1: Bitterness, I often tell myself "if only that hadn't happened"
q1.opt2: Sadness sometimes, but I no longer think about it constantly
q1.opt3: Quiet acceptance, it's my story and part of who I am
q1.opt4: Full affirmation: I wouldn't want it not to have happened

q2.text: Toward people who hurt you in the past, what is your attitude?
q2.opt0: I hold deep grudges, I sometimes imagine taking revenge
q2.opt1: I still resent them a lot, I avoid thinking about it because it makes me angry
q2.opt2: I'm working on forgiving them, but it's hard
q2.opt3: I don't really hold grudges anymore, I've moved on
q2.opt4: I accept them as part of my story, without resentment or need for forgiveness

q3.text: When you go through a difficult trial (illness, failure, loss), what is your dominant reaction?
q3.opt0: "Why me? It's unfair!" - I feel like a victim
q3.opt1: "I have to turn this into something positive so it has meaning"
q3.opt2: "It's hard, but it's part of life, I'll get through it"
q3.opt3: "It's a trial, I accept it and will go through it with dignity"
q3.opt4: "This suffering is part of my life, I say yes even to that"

q4.text: How do you react when something is completely out of your control?
q4.opt0: I get annoyed, I fight reality, I refuse to accept it
q4.opt1: I'm frustrated but eventually resign myself reluctantly
q4.opt2: I accept it fairly quickly and focus on what I can control
q4.opt3: I accept it calmly, understanding not everything depends on me
q4.opt4: I welcome even that as part of the flow of life

q5.text: Regarding your important life choices (work, relationships, projects), you'd say they are:
q5.opt0: Mainly dictated by others' expectations (family, society)
q5.opt1: A mix, but I struggle to distinguish what truly comes from me
q5.opt2: Fairly aligned with my values, even if I sometimes compromise
q5.opt3: Clearly aligned with my personal values, I own my choices
q5.opt4: I create my own values and live by them, regardless of judgment

q6.text: If you were completely honest: do you accept who you really are (strengths AND weaknesses)?
q6.opt0: No, I hate myself or reject parts of who I am
q6.opt1: I accept my strengths but fight against my weaknesses
q6.opt2: I'm learning to accept myself, it's a work in progress
q6.opt3: Yes, I accept who I am as a whole
q6.opt4: I fully affirm who I am, including my shadow sides

q7.text: In your daily life, you'd say you spend most of your time:
q7.opt0: Enduring and complaining about what happens to me
q7.opt2: Managing my obligations with a little time for myself
q7.opt3: Actively building projects I care about
q7.opt4: Creating (art, thought, relationships, works) with passion and necessity

q8.text: Faced with failure or difficulty, you tend to:
q8.opt0: Collapse and give up, remain paralyzed
q8.opt1: Look for someone to blame (others, circumstances, yourself)
q8.opt2: Step back, learn, and try again
q8.opt3: Use the failure as material to create something new
q8.opt4: Fully accept it and create from this new reality

q9.text: If you had to relive your life EXACTLY as it is (same joys, same suffering, same mistakes), what would you say?
q9.opt0: No, absolutely not! My life has too much suffering and failure
q9.opt1: No, I'd want to change many things
q9.opt2: Maybe... if I could change a few key events
q9.opt3: Probably yes, even though some moments were very hard
q9.opt4: Yes, absolutely, without changing a single thing

q10.text: Honestly, why would you (or not) accept reliving your life?
q10.opt0: I wouldn't accept it, there's been too much pointless suffering
q10.opt1: Only if the suffering "served" some purpose
q10.opt2: Because the good moments outweigh the bad ones
q10.opt3: Because it's my life, with everything it contains
q10.opt4: Because I affirm the totality of existence, even the tragic

0.title: 🌑 Nihilism & Resentment (0-8 points)
0.text0: You're currently in a phase of strong reactivity to your life. Resentment and refusal of what is consume much of your energy.
0.text1: This is normal and human. Many people go through it. What matters is recognizing where you are so you can move forward.
0.text2: Start by identifying just one thing in your life you could accept this week, without trying to justify or change it.

1.title: 🌘 Resignation & Struggle (9-16 points)
1.text0: You oscillate between forced acceptance and inner struggle. Part of you wants to let go, another part still resists.
1.text1: This transitional phase takes courage. You're beginning to see there could be another way to live.
1.text2: Focus on the dimensions where your score is lowest - that's where the work will be most transformative.

2.title: 🌗 Acceptance Under Construction (17-24 points)
2.text0: You're on a good path. You've already integrated certain forms of acceptance, even if it's not always easy.
2.text1: Amor fati isn't fully lived yet, but you already understand its logic and apply it at times.
2.text2: Keep up the work. Notice in which situations you fall back into resentment, and why.

3.title: 🌖 Advanced Affirmation (25-32 points)
3.text0: You've reached a significant level of amor fati. You accept much of your life without needing to justify it.
3.text1: There may remain a few zones of resistance - that's normal. What matters is that you actively create rather than react.
3.text2: You're probably already an inspiration to others, even without knowing it. Keep deepening.

4.title: 🌕 Amor Fati Fulfilled (33-40 points)
4.text0: You embody amor fati at a rare level. You affirm your life totally, even in its hardest aspects.
4.text1: Be careful not to fall into pride or the performance of the "strong person". True amor fati stays humble.
4.text2: Your very presence can help others see that another relationship to life is possible. Share what you live, without proselytizing.

ressentiment.0: Write a letter (you won't send) to someone who hurt you. Then symbolically burn it.
ressentiment.1: Practice the exercise: 'What if this person did exactly what they had to do for me to become who I am?'
ressentiment.2: Journal: each evening, note a painful past event and write: 'I say yes to this part of my story.'

souffrance.0: Faced with a difficulty this week, ask yourself: 'How can I create something from this?'
souffrance.1: Practice the Stoic distinction: list what depends on you vs. what doesn't.
souffrance.2: Meditation on impermanence: everything passes, even this suffering. Can you accept it while it lasts?

authenticite.0: Identify a decision you make out of fear of judgment. Can you do otherwise this week?
authenticite.1: List 5 values that truly define you. Do your life choices reflect them?
authenticite.2: Exercise: for a day, observe how often you censor yourself or play a role.

creation.0: Replace one hour of consumption with one hour of creation (of anything).
creation.1: Faced with a problem, ask yourself: 'What can I create from this constraint?'
creation.2: Launch a small creative project this week, without waiting for it to be perfect.

eternel.0: Practice the eternal return exercise: visualize your life repeating. What makes you say no? Why?
eternel.1: List 3 aspects of your life you'd want to relive eternally. Then 3 you'd refuse. Explore why.
eternel.2: Each evening: 'Would I want to relive this day eternally?' If not, what should change?

none.0: Simply observe your progress without judging yourself. The path matters as much as the destination.
none.1: Focus on your weakest dimension (see above).
